tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and elevated creatinine, suggesting possible chronic kidney dysfunction. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible renal impairment and metabolic derangement. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, and further evaluation for underlying causes (e.g., diabetes, liver dysfunction, or acute illness).

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
